Test Steps:
1. Open Postman
2. Set method to POST
3. Enter URL
4. Go to Body → raw → JSON
5. Paste request body
6. Click Send

Test Case Description: Try sign up with existing email
Test Steps:
1. Use email that was already registered
2. Send request

1. Omit email field
2. Send request
Expected Result: success=false; error message about missing email

Test Steps:
1. Set method to POST
2. Enter correct credentials
3. Click Send
Expected Result: success=true; data contains token and userId
Notes: ⚠️ IMPORTANT: Copy token for next requests

Test Steps:
1. Use correct email but wrong password
2. Send request

1. Use email that doesn't exist
2. Send request
Expected Result: success=false; error about user not found

Test Steps:
1. Add token to Authorization header
2. Set method to POST
3. Send request (no body)

1. Use valid token in header
2. Send GET request
Expected Result: success=true; data contains userId; data.username matches Login

Test Case Description:
1. Set method to GET
2. No authentication needed
3. Send request
Test Steps: success=true; data is array; contains table objects

Test Case Description:
1. Add query parameter: ?status=available
2. Send GET request
Test Steps: success=true; all tables have status='available'

Test Case Description:
1. Add query parameter: ?capacity=4
2. Send GET request
Test Steps: success=true; all returned tables have capacity>=4
